postData.contentHtml:
For over a decade, Chick-fil-A held the crown as the undisputed leader in customer satisfaction among fast-food chains. However, a new study reveals a significant shift in the quick-service restaurant (QSR) landscape: Jersey Mike's Subs has officially claimed the top spot.

The latest American Customer Satisfaction Index (ACSI) report, compiled from 16,464 customer surveys conducted between April 2025 and March 2026, awarded Jersey Mike's an impressive score of 84 out of 100. Chick-fil-A, while still highly regarded, slipped to second place with a score of 83.

Jersey Mike's rise to the top is attributed to its consistent high customer satisfaction scores amidst rapid expansion. The report highlights the chain's successful business model, which fosters franchisee success and leverages high digital pickup usage, making convenience a key factor in its appeal. Founded in Point Pleasant, New Jersey, in 1956, Jersey Mike's is renowned for its freshly sliced, made-to-order subs, often served "Mike's Way" with onions, lettuce, tomatoes, oil, vinegar, and spices.

While Chick-fil-A may have lost its overall number one position, the ACSI report emphasizes that it remains the "clear leader for chicken." This indicates that despite the change at the very top, the Atlanta-based chain continues to dominate its niche.

Beyond the top two, the ACSI ranking provides a snapshot of other prominent fast-food players:

  • Tied for third place: Jimmy John's and Panda Express, both scoring 81.
  • Following closely: KFC, Papa Johns, and Pizza Hut, each with a score of 80.
  • Next in line: Domino's, Raising Cane's, Starbucks, and Subway, all earning ratings of 79.
  • Further down the list: Burger King, Culver's, Dunkin', Little Caesars, and Panera Bread, with scores of 78.
  • Ranking near the bottom among major quick-service chains: Dairy Queen and McDonald’s, both receiving scores of 72.

On the social media front, both Jersey Mike's and Chick-fil-A generally enjoy positive sentiment, though online discussions reveal ongoing debates and diverse opinions surrounding both brands. This suggests a passionate customer base for both, even as the tides of top satisfaction shift.

This change at the top signifies a dynamic and competitive fast-food industry where customer experience, innovative business models, and consistent quality are key to maintaining a leading edge.
postData.title: A New King in Fast Food: Jersey Mike's Dethrones Chick-fil-A
postData.summary: After more than a decade, Chick-fil-A has been unseated as America's favorite fast-food chain, with Jersey Mike's Subs taking the top spot in customer satisfaction.

sources.title: Chick-fil-A's reign is over. See who is now the top fast food chain
Which fast-food chain have Americans crowned the new champ? And what fan-favorite lost the throne?
